data.description: A strong protest has been lodged against the German ambassador over a meeting between a Bundestag deputy and the head of a terrorist organization banned in Russia, the Russian Foreign Ministry announced.

data.title: onsemi and NIO Expand Strategic Collaboration to Accelerate Next-Generation 900V EV Platforms
data.description: NIO's latest EV platforms are equipped with EliteSiC technology, enhancing performance with faster charging, longer range, and stronger capabilities. This advanced technology has significantly improved the vehicles' charging speed, increasing efficiency and enabling longer travel distances. By incorporating EliteSiC technology, NIO's EVs demonstrate superior performance compared to previous models, ultimately offering an enhanced driving experience to consumers.

data.title: CPI Inflation Turns Cold, Though Data Has Issues; S&P 500 Rallies
data.description: Despite a more dovish outlook from the Federal Reserve, stocks have not seen a significant increase. The latest data on CPI inflation shows a decrease, but there are concerns about its accuracy. Despite this, the S&P 500 has experienced a rally in response.

data.title: Jobs Report: Unemployment Jumps But Fed Cut Will Wait; S&P 500 Slips
data.description: In November, the United States saw a rise of 64,000 new jobs being added to the economy. Despite this positive growth, the unemployment rate also increased to 4.6% following the end of the government shutdown. The latest jobs report indicates a mixed outlook, with the Federal Reserve expected to wait on making any cuts despite the rise in unemployment. Additionally, the S&P 500 experienced a slight decline during this period.

data.title: Fed Cuts Key Rate To Stem Job Losses; S&P 500 Rises
data.description: The Federal Reserve has reduced its main interest rate in response to current economic conditions, with the S&P 500 responding to projections for the year 2026 and statements made by Chair Jerome Powell during a news conference. This move comes as a measure to mitigate job losses. The post-Fed action led to an increase in the S&P 500 index.

data.title: Fed Meeting: Job Openings Jump, But Don't Worry About Hawkish Shift
data.description: The latest Federal Reserve projections suggest that there may be only one interest rate cut in 2026. The context of this update is crucial as it follows the recent Federal Reserve meeting where job openings experienced a significant increase. Despite this potentially hawkish shift in monetary policy, experts advise against concerns and emphasize the need for a comprehensive understanding of the situation. Stay informed with the latest updates in the financial market.

data.title: Key Fed Inflation Rate Makes Rate Cut A Lock; S&P 500 Rallies
data.description: The current inflation rate is lower than the Federal Reserve's predicted rate. This development has now increased the likelihood of a rate cut by the Fed. This news has positively impacted the S&P 500, leading to a rally in the stock market.

data.title: ADP Jobs Report Shows Surprise Drop In Private Payrolls; S&P 500 Wavers
data.description: The official Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) employment report will be released after the Federal Reserve meeting. Prior to this, the ADP jobs report indicated an unexpected decrease in private payrolls. As a result, the S&P 500 experienced fluctuations in response to this news.

data.title: LAGENIO Enters the U.S. Market with K9 and Advances a New Era of AI Powered Kids Smart Watch
data.description: LAGENIO made its debut in the U.S. on April 27, 2026, unveiling the K9, a smartwatch designed for kids aged 5-12. This device is the first of its kind, utilizing AI technology and 4G capabilities. The K9 offers unique features such as AI-assisted learning and various family communication modes, setting a new standard for kids' smartwatches globally.

data.title: Robinhood Q1 Earnings Preview: Will The Vlad Tenev-Led Brokerage Firm Feel The Pinch Of Crypto Bear Market?
data.description: Robinhood Markets Inc. is set to report its first-quarter earnings after the closing bell on Tuesday. Analysts project earnings per share to be $0.39, marking a 5.4% increase from the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is estimated to be $1.14 billion, showing a significant rise from $927 million in the previous year's quarter, although a decrease of about 11% from the prior quarter. A consensus of 27 analysts sets the price target for HOOD stock at $109.70, with the highest forecast at $170 by JMP Securities. Recent adjustments from JPMorgan and KeyBanc have notably lowered their price targets. For the full news story, please visit the Benzinga website.
